title: International Security - Journal Highlights
summary: Highlights from the International Security Journal.
body: Vol. 44 No. 2 Fall 2019 Following are brief summaries of articles in the current issue of the journal International Security. China in a World of Orders: Rethinking Compliance and Challenge in Beijing’s International Relations Alastair Iain Johnston Rather than debating whether China is challenging a single, U.S.-dominated liberal order, scholars and analysts should consider China’s actions in relation to multiple orders in different domains, where China is supportive of some, unsupportive of others, and partially supportive of still others. Dangerous Confidence? Chinese Views on Nuclear Escalation Fiona Cunningham and M. Taylor Fravel Maintaining the liberal international order is central in the debate over U.S. security and foreign policy. Much of what the liberal order purports to explain, however, can be explained by other theories. Analyzing issues through a grandstrategic lens would provide broader options for achieving U.S. interests. Home, Again: Refugee Return and Post-Conflict Violence in Burundi Stephanie Schwartz In post-conflict societies, divisions can emerge between returnee and nonmigrant groups, which can in turn lead to violence and destabilization when government institutions favor one group over another. The Domestic Politics of Nuclear Choices— A Review Essay Elizabeth N. Saunders Domestic politics play a significant role in nuclear decisionmaking under two conditions: when uncertainty over the nature and intensity of a threat is high, and when leaders are incentivized to centralize nuclear policymaking. The journal International Security is edited at Harvard Kennedy School’s Belfer Center and published quarterly by the MIT Press. Questions may be directed to IS@hks.harvard.edu. Follow us on Twitter @journal_is Compiled by International Security staff The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "International Security - Journal Highlights." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. (Fall/Winter 2019-2020). _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: This article provides summaries of several academic papers from the Fall 2019 issue of the journal International Security. The first summary discusses China's complex relationship with global orders, arguing that its behavior varies across different domains rather than representing a monolithic challenge. The second examines Chinese perspectives on nuclear escalation dynamics. A third piece analyzes the potential for violence between returning refugees and non-migrant populations in post-conflict Burundi. Another review essay highlights the significant role of domestic politics in nuclear decision-making, particularly under conditions of high threat uncertainty and centralized policymaking. The journal is edited at Harvard Kennedy School's Belfer Center and published by MIT Press, focusing on international security and defense issues.
result.main_content_zh: 本文摘录了《国际安全》期刊2019年秋季刊（第44卷第2期）中多篇学术文章的概要。首篇概要提出，应超越中国是否挑战单一美国主导的自由主义秩序的辩论，转而分析中国在多个不同领域秩序中的行为，其态度可能是支持、不支持或部分支持。第二篇概要探讨了中国对核升级的看法。第三篇研究了布隆迪后冲突社会中，返乡难民与非移民群体之间可能因政府偏袒而产生的分裂与暴力风险。另一篇评论文章则指出，在国内政治存在高度威胁不确定性和决策集中化激励两种条件下，其对核决策的影响尤为显著。该期刊由哈佛肯尼迪学院贝尔弗中心编辑，麻省理工学院出版社出版，核心关注国际安全与防务议题。
